executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated significantly in the past 24 hours with confirmed US combat casualties (3 killed, 5 wounded) and Iranian attacks disabling US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. IRGC claims it struck three oil tankers in the Persian Gulf and struck the USS Abraham Lincoln (denied by Pentagon). European powers France, UK, and Germany announced joint 'defensive measures' against Iran. The death of Supreme Leader Khamenei has triggered Iranian retaliation across the region, with attacks on Israel, Gulf states, and Iraq. Threat level remains MAJOR with 726 incidents in 24 hours.
whats_new: IRGC claims complete destruction of Ali Al-Salem US naval base in Kuwait and strike on Salman Port base in Bahrain
Iranian missile/drone attack causes massive fire at US base in Qatar - direct targeting of US forces in Gulf
US Central Command confirms 3 killed, 5 injured (8 total) - first confirmed American combat deaths
IRGC claims strike on 3 US/UK oil tankers attempting to exit Persian Gulf - energy supply threat
UAE closes Tehran embassy and withdraws diplomatic staff - Gulf state severs ties
Trump claims 48 Iranian leaders killed, 9 Iranian naval ships sunk
UK Foreign Office planning mass evacuation of tens of thousands of Britons from Middle East
France, UK, Germany announce joint 'defensive measures' - European powers formally join military campaign
Source claims Strait of Hormuz closure will apply to all ships - major escalation threat
Trump says Iran is negotiating with US - unverified claim requiring confirmation
confirmed_events: US Central Command confirms 3 US service members killed, 5 wounded in Iranian ballistic missile/drone strikes on Gulf bases - first American combat deaths
Iranian ballistic missile strike on Beit Shemesh, Israel killed at least 9 people with 11 missing - highest single-day Israeli casualties
US B-2 stealth bombers struck Iranian ballistic missile facilities with 2,000-pound bombs - confirmed by CENTCOM and Trump
UAE Capital Markets Authority ordered Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange and Dubai Financial Market closed March 2-3 following Iran strikes
Iranian missiles and drones struck US base in Erbil, Iraqi Kurdistan - direct attack on US forces in Iraq
unverified_emerging: Iranian Education Ministry claims 153 female students killed in US-Israeli attack on Minab school - requires independent verification
IRGC claims 4 cruise missiles targeted USS Abraham Lincoln, causing it to flee to Indian Ocean - Pentagon denies, single-source claim
IRGC claims to have struck 3 American and British oil tankers in Persian Gulf - unconfirmed details on casualties/damage
Iran launched fissile (nuclear-capable) missiles in latest strike on Israel - Al-Mayadeen report, unverified
IRGC claims 40 killed, 60 wounded Zionists in Haifa from new wave of IRGC attacks - Hezbollah Electronic Army source, requires verification
Trump claims Iran is negotiating with US - RBC/NBC report, unconfirmed by Iranian side
Attack on Tehran's Grand Bazaar - Press TV images show aftermath, civilian area struck

people_travel: UK Foreign Office drawing up evacuation plans for tens of thousands of British citizens in Middle East
Turkish border with Iran reports citizens trapped attempting to flee - border congestion at Khoy gate
supply_chain: UAE stock markets closed March 2-3 - regional financial markets disrupted
IRGC threats to close Strait of Hormuz to all ships - global oil supply route at risk
Three oil tankers reportedly struck in Persian Gulf - potential supply chain disruption if confirmed
market_macro: UAE markets closed for two days following Iran strikes - Gulf economic disruption
Energy markets on edge as Iran war threatens oil supplies through Strait of Hormuz
Global oil prices likely to spike on Persian Gulf tanker attacks and Strait threat

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is approaching a critical inflection point with confirmed US casualties triggering domestic political pressure. Iranian retaliation continues across multiple fronts (Gulf bases, Israel, Iraq) while US大规模打击 targets伊朗军事设施. European powers joining the campaign expands the conflict beyond bilateral US-Iran hostilities. The 24-72h trajectory points to continued escalation unless diplomatic channels produce breakthrough, with Iranian threats to close Strait of Hormuz representing the most significant supply chain risk.
escalation_risks: Strait of Hormuz closure would cripple global oil supply - 20% of world oil passes through
Iranian use of nuclear-capable missiles (if confirmed) represents massive escalation
Sleeper cells activation in US or Gulf states - counterterrorism threat
Direct IRGC attacks on Israeli cities escalating to full-scale war
US-Israeli disagreement on campaign objectives - Trump impatience vs Israel maximizing gains
Energy infrastructure attacks spreading to Saudi Arabia or other Gulf states
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: Trump's claim that Iran is negotiating could signal back-channel diplomacy opening. European involvement (France, UK, Germany) may provide diplomatic off-ramp if Iran leadership succession stabilizes. However, Khamenei's death removes a key figure for negotiation, and Iranian FM statement Iran will defend its people 'without ceiling or limits' suggests Tehran not seeking immediate de-escalation. The 48-72h window is critical - if Strait threats materialize or further US casualties occur, conflict becomes self-sustaining.
